message:
fcp-webhook: harden Slack + Linear signature verification edges

Audit sweep of crates/fcp-webhook signature verification. Pre-existing
on-branch fixes covered GitHub/Stripe body caps and Stripe header-field
length caps. Remaining gaps closed here:

- [MEDIUM] Slack verify_and_parse: cap body size before building the
  `v0:{timestamp}:{body}` signing base string (second allocation of
  comparable size to the body).
- [MEDIUM] Linear verify_and_parse: cap body size before HMAC runs so
  an attacker cannot amplify CPU cost with an unbounded payload.
- Linear replay-protection doc mirroring the GitHub warning: Linear
  deliveries carry no timestamp covered by the HMAC, so captured
  deliveries remain valid indefinitely absent caller-side idempotency
  tracking.
- Added `with_timestamp_tolerance` builder on Slack so callers can
  pin the replay window.

Clean findings (no patch needed):
- All HMAC compares go through `mac.verify_slice` (constant-time).
- `header_value_case_insensitive` uses `eq_ignore_ascii_case`.
- Stripe timestamp uses `abs_diff` so future-dated replays fail too.
- Stripe caps `v1=` list at 10 entries; `t=`/`v1=` value lengths
  bounded before `to_string()` allocation.

Regression tests added for both new body-size caps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
